title: Getting Started with QuotaLens — A Practical Guide for Mobile Developers
description: Step-by-step guide to getting started with QuotaLens. Learn setup, configuration, and best practices for Mobile.

sourceData.name: QuotaLens
sourceData.description: See your AI usage clearly — a macOS menu-bar gauge for Claude & Codex quotas.

bodyHtml:
QuotaLens is making waves in the Mobile ecosystem this week, gaining 6 new stars. With a total of 58 stars on GitHub and 1 forks, this open-source project is rapidly becoming a go-to tool for developers worldwide.

⭐ 58Stars
📈 +6/wkGrowth
🔀 1Forks
⚠️ 0Open Issues

Key Features

  • Written in Swift for maximum performance
  • Licensed under MIT — free to use and modify
  • Official website: https://quotalens.fun
  • 🏷️ claude
  • 🏷️ codex
  • 🏷️ macos
  • 🏷️ menu-bar
  • 🏷️ qqhr

Why QuotaLens Is Trending

The Mobile landscape is constantly evolving, and QuotaLens has emerged as one of the fastest-growing projects this week. Several factors contribute to its momentum:

  1. Growing community: With 6 new stars this week alone, developer adoption is accelerating rapidly.
  2. Active maintenance: The project sees regular updates, ensuring compatibility with the latest ecosystem changes.
  3. Practical utility: See your AI usage clearly — a macOS menu-bar gauge for Claude & Codex quotas.

If you're working in Mobile, this is definitely a project worth watching — and adopting.

Practical Use Cases

Here are some common scenarios where QuotaLens shines:

  1. New projects: Start your next Swift project with this tool as a foundation
  2. Migration: Consider switching if your current solution is slowing you down
  3. Learning: Study the source code to understand modern Mobile patterns

Quick Start

Get started with QuotaLens in minutes:

# Clone the repository git clone https://github.com/mangiapanejohn-dev/QuotaLens # Navigate to project cd QuotaLens # Check the README for detailed setup instructions

For full documentation and advanced usage, visit the official GitHub repository.

Final Thoughts

QuotaLens is a solid addition to any developer's toolkit. With its growing community, active development, and practical design, it's well worth your time to evaluate. Star it on GitHub, try it in a side project, and see if it fits your workflow.
